title: John Brown: Correspondence relating to John Brown and the raid on Harpers Ferry, West Virginia
creator: Boston Public Library; multiple correspondents
rights_status: No known restrictions indicated by repository
attribution_caveat: Collection includes John Brown and associated correspondents; item-level authorship and date review required
notes: Digital Commonwealth collection page says the collection contains correspondence on Brown and the raid, dates 1847-1904 with bulk 1858-1860, correspondents including John Brown, and no known restrictions. Search/list access encountered browser verification when opening all items; use repository/API or manual item access.

title: Provisional Constitution and Ordinances for the People of the United States Written by John Brown
creator: John Brown
rights_status: Public Domain, Free of Known Copyright Restrictions per DocsTeach/National Archives page
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch via DocsTeach page, WordPress media metadata, and page-image URLs; no crawling or access-control bypass
authorship_status: attributed to Brown by DocsTeach/National Archives metadata
attribution_caveat: National Archives Identifier 3819337; quote-level verification against page images required before evidence use
notes: DocsTeach metadata preserved with nine-page image set and media JSON. Selected consistent 2025-10 page-image set; media JSON also lists duplicate page-1 and older document-path uploads. OCR generated locally and is noisy; page 09 OCR is empty, likely because the scanned page contains little or no machine-readable text. NARA Catalog API follow-up returned SPA HTML rather than JSON, so DocsTeach metadata is the preserved acquisition witness.

title: [Extracts from letters by John Brown] [manuscript]
creator: John Brown; copied by Samuel May
rights_status: Internet Archive/Boston Public Library public text item; item metadata has no explicit rights or license field
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch from Internet Archive metadata and advertised item files; no crawling or access-control bypass
authorship_status: copied extracts of Brown originals in Samuel May hand per item metadata
attribution_caveat: Not Brown autograph manuscripts; cataloger-supplied title and Samuel May copy status require quotation-level verification against scan images/PDF
notes: Preserved IA metadata JSON, Text PDF, DjVu OCR text, and DjVu XML. Initial guessed filenames returned 404 before metadata-advertised filenames were used. OCR is extremely noisy because the item is manuscript handwriting; use for discovery only until manually verified.

title: John Brown's Statement (1859)
creator: John Brown; reported courtroom statement
rights_status: Educational web transcription; rights/source chain require review before reuse beyond discovery
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch of web page for discovery only; no access-control bypass
attribution_caveat: Derivative transcription; compare against contemporary newspaper and pamphlet witnesses
notes: Preserved derivative transcription page for source discovery and comparison only. Not approved as source substitution and not quotation-final; compare against SRC-0010 and contemporary witnesses.

title: The Trial of John Brown source guide and transcripts
creator: John Brown; court reporters; editor Douglas O. Linder
rights_status: Educational web transcription/source guide; rights/source chain require review before reuse beyond discovery
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch of web page for discovery only; no access-control bypass
attribution_caveat: Derivative site; use to identify witnesses and compare wording, not as sole thesis-bearing source
notes: Preserved derivative source-guide/transcription page for discovery and comparison only. Not approved as source substitution and not quotation-final; compare against SRC-0010 and contemporary witnesses.

title: Records of the Senate Select Committee to Inquire into the Facts Attending the Invasion and Seizure of the United States Armory at Harpers Ferry
creator: United States Senate
rights_status: National Archives public web guide; federal finding-aid page preserved for discovery/provenance
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch of National Archives guide page; no access-control bypass
attribution_caveat: Not Brown-authored; contextual and reported-statement evidence only
notes: Preserved NARA guide page and normalized text. Guide identifies Senate Select Committee records and Microfilm Publication M1196; it is a pointer to records, not itself Brown-authored evidence.

title: John Brown collection
creator: John Brown; family and associates
repository: Robert W. Woodruff Library, Atlanta University Center
rights_status: Finding aid public; underlying collection rights statement says materials are protected by copyright and/or property of AUC Woodruff Library and/or copyright holder
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch of finding-aid overview, inventory, and digitized-materials listing pages; no access-control bypass
attribution_caveat: Finding aid page triggered validation/access behavior in browser; item access must respect repository controls
notes: Preserved public finding-aid pages and normalized discovery text. Rights statement requires contact for underlying materials; item-level manuscript acquisition from this collection is blocked unless public rights and download terms are established or the scholar approves repository-contact workflow.

title: Copies of correspondence to and from John Brown
creator: John Brown; Ruth Thompson; Samuel May Jr. annotator/copy context
rights_status: No known copyright restrictions; No known restrictions on use per Digital Commonwealth metadata and IIIF manifest
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch from documented Digital Commonwealth JSON and IIIF endpoints plus advertised text derivative; no access-control bypass
authorship_status: repository metadata lists Brown as author/addressee and Samuel May Jr. as annotator; transcription/copy hands require review
attribution_caveat: The item is copies/transcriptions, not necessarily Brown autograph; annotations are from May and transcriptions/annotations are not the same hand per metadata
notes: Added as item-level acquisition under SRC-0001 collection. Human-facing page returned browser verification in web view, but Digital Commonwealth API documentation supports .json and IIIF manifest endpoints. Preserved four page images, item JSON, IIIF manifest, transcription JSON, and text_plain derivative.

title: Harpers Ferry Investigation, 1860
creator: United States Senate Select Committee to Inquire into the Facts Attending the Invasion and Seizure of the United States Armory at Harpers Ferry
rights_status: United States Senate public PDF; federal government publication scan
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch of Senate PDF; text extracted locally with pdftotext; no access-control bypass
authorship_status: government report and testimony, not Brown-authored
attribution_caveat: Use as government/reported contextual witness only; Brown statements and testimony require locator-level verification against the PDF scan
notes: Added as stronger public witness for the NARA M1196/Senate investigation branch identified by SRC-0006. PDF metadata reports copy disabled, but pdftotext extraction succeeded; normalized text is OCR-like and imperfect.

title: Testimonies of Capt. John Brown, at Harper's Ferry: with his address to the court
creator: John Brown; American Anti-Slavery Society publisher
repository: Internet Archive / Oberlin College Library / American Anti-Slavery Society tract
rights_status: Internet Archive public text item; item metadata has no explicit rights or license field; 1860 publication likely public domain but verify before reuse claims
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch from Internet Archive metadata and advertised PDF/DjVu derivatives; no crawling or access-control bypass
authorship_status: published posthumous pamphlet witness attributed to Brown for address/letters; editorial/publisher framing by American Anti-Slavery Society
attribution_caveat: Not a stenographic court original; use as posthumous published witness and compare against contemporary newspaper/government witnesses before quotation-final use
notes: Added as stronger public witness for Brown courtroom-address branch than modern derivative pages. Preserved IA metadata JSON, Text PDF, DjVu OCR text, and DjVu XML. OCR must be checked against PDF images.

title: John Brown autograph letter to Dear Friend and Other Friends, Osawatomie, Kansas, 10 & 13 September 1858
creator: John Brown
rights_status: No known copyright restrictions; No known restrictions on use per Digital Commonwealth metadata and IIIF manifest
acquisition_method: source-specific direct public fetch from documented Digital Commonwealth JSON and IIIF endpoints; local OCR generated; no access-control bypass
authorship_status: autograph letter attributed to Brown by repository metadata
attribution_caveat: Repository metadata says printed in F. B. Sanborn, The Life and Letters of John Brown, p. 477-78; transcription must be verified against page images
notes: Digital Commonwealth metadata lists Brown as author and local call number MS E.5.1, pt. 1, p. 39. No repository transcription advertised. Local manuscript OCR is noisy and discovery-only.
